---
format:
  dashboard:
    embed-resources: false
    theme:
      - default
      - lib/custom.scss
    nav-buttons: []
    orientation: rows
params:
  all_data_path: ""
  render_ts: ""
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

proj_root <- normalizePath("..", winslash = "/")
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = proj_root)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(ggplot2); library(plotly); library(DT); library(htmltools)
  library(jsonlite)
})
for (f in c("utils.R", "data_layer.R", "portfolio_runner.R",
            "comparison_engine.R", "ai_interpret.R", "plot_helpers.R",
            "html_components.R")) {
  source(file.path(proj_root, "R", f), local = FALSE)
}

all_data <- readRDS(params$all_data_path)
all_data <- Filter(Negate(is.null), all_data)
stopifnot(length(all_data) >= 1)

primary   <- all_data[[length(all_data)]]   # latest cohort by order of entry
render_ts <- if (nzchar(params$render_ts %||% "")) {
  params$render_ts
} else {
  format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
}

overall_medians <- vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$overall_median %||% NA_real_, numeric(1))
total_students  <- sum(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$total_students  %||% 0L, integer(1)))
total_periods   <- sum(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$periods_covered %||% 0L, integer(1)))

# Period index — feeds the sticky filter bar. Serialize to JSON for JS.
period_index       <- build_period_index(all_data)
period_index_json  <- jsonlite::toJSON(period_index, auto_unbox = TRUE, null = "null")

# Helper: per-cohort data-period-keys attribute, space-separated. The
# filter JS shows/hides any element whose data-period-keys overlaps the
# active period set. For all_data entries with multiple periods, we list
# all period IDs under that cohort.
cohort_period_keys <- function(r) {
  if (is.null(r) || length(r$periods) == 0) return("")
  slug <- cohort_slug(r$cohort_label)
  paste(sprintf("%d_%s", as.integer(r$periods), slug), collapse = " ")
}
```

---
title: "`r sprintf('%s — %s (All years)', primary$course_code, primary$course_name)`"
---

# {.sidebar}

```{r}
cohort_labels <- vapply(all_data, function(r) r$cohort_label, character(1))
ts_names <- if (!is.null(primary$tag_coverage) && length(primary$tag_coverage) > 0) {
  pretty_tagset_name(names(primary$tag_coverage[[1]] %||% list()))
} else character(0)

ui_sidebar(
  course_code = primary$course_code,
  course_name = primary$course_name,
  metadata = c(
    sprintf("Combined view — %d classes", length(all_data)),
    sprintf("%d periods total", total_periods),
    sprintf("Rendered %s", render_ts)
  ),
  extra_sections = list(
    list(title = "Classes included",    items = cohort_labels),
    list(title = "Tag sets available",  items = ts_names)
  )
)
```

# Overview

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Portfolio at a glance"
# Every KPI card is tagged with data-kpi — the filter JS recomputes the .value
# text on every chip change. Initial values reflect ALL periods selected.
tagList(
  ui_kpi_row(
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Pooled median",
      value    = if (any(!is.na(overall_medians))) sprintf("%.1f", median(overall_medians, na.rm = TRUE)) else "—",
      subvalue = "Median across selected periods",
      kpi_key  = "median"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Students assessed",
      value    = fmt_num(total_students),
      subvalue = "Summed across selected periods",
      kpi_key  = "students"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Periods covered",
      value    = fmt_num(total_periods),
      kpi_key  = "periods"
    ),
    ui_kpi_card(
      label    = "Tag sets",
      value    = fmt_num(max(vapply(all_data, function(r) r$kpi$n_tag_sets %||% 0L, integer(1)))),
      subvalue = "Distinct curriculum tag sets",
      kpi_key  = "tag_sets"
    )
  )
)
```

```{r}
#| title: "Data completeness"
rows <- lapply(all_data, function(r) {
  data.frame(
    cohort        = r$cohort_label,
    gradebook_ok  = isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok),
    n_periods     = length(r$periods),
    n_students    = r$kpi$total_students %||% 0L,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})
status_df <- do.call(rbind, rows)
tagList(
  ui_section_header("Completeness by cohort"),
  DT::datatable(
    status_df, rownames = FALSE,
    options = list(dom = "t", pageLength = 20)
  ) |>
    DT::formatStyle("gradebook_ok",
                    backgroundColor = DT::styleEqual(c(TRUE, FALSE),
                                                     c("#E8F5E9", "#FFEBEE")))
)
```

# Assessment Performance

```{r}
#| output: asis
emit_html <- function(tag) {
  if (is.null(tag)) return(invisible())
  cat("\n```{=html}\n", as.character(tag), "\n```\n\n", sep = "")
}

cat("## {.tabset}\n\n")
for (r in all_data) {
  cat(sprintf("### %s\n\n", r$cohort_label))

  # Wrap this cohort's entire tab content in a .cvm-filterable div so the
  # filter JS can show/hide the whole subtree when no periods of this
  # cohort are selected.
  keys <- cohort_period_keys(r)
  emit_html(HTML(sprintf(
    '<div class="cvm-filterable" data-period-keys="%s">',
    htmlEscape(keys)
  )))

  if (!isTRUE(r$gradebook_ok)) {
    emit_html(ui_banner(
      text = sprintf(
        "Gradebook data unavailable for %s. Cross-year overlay still includes this cohort's tag coverage.",
        htmlEscape(r$cohort_label)
      ),
      variant = "warn"
    ))
  } else {
    emit_html(ui_section_header("Median grade trend"))
    print(trend_plotly(median_trend(r$grades_df),
                       title = sprintf("Median grade trend — %s", r$cohort_label),
                       height = 380))

    if (!is.null(r$assessment_plots) && !is.null(r$assessment_plots$median)) {
      emit_html(ui_section_header("Median by assessment"))
      print(plotly::ggplotly(r$assessment_plots$median, height = 500) |>
              plotly::config(displaylogo = FALSE, responsive = TRUE))
    }
    if (!is.null(r$assessment_plots) && !is.null(r$assessment_plots$pct)) {
      emit_html(ui_section_header("Counts below 70% / 80% / 90%"))
      print(plotly::ggplotly(r$assessment_plots$pct, height = 500) |>
              plotly::config(displaylogo = FALSE, responsive = TRUE))
    }
  }

  emit_html(HTML("</div>"))
}
```

# Cross-Year Comparison

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Grade trend overlay"
overlay <- build_grade_overlay(all_data)
tagList(
  ui_section_header("Median trend across cohorts"),
  if (nrow(overlay) == 0) {
    tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472; padding:24px;",
           "Not enough grade data to build an overlay.")
  } else {
    plotly::plot_ly(
      overlay, x = ~seq, y = ~median, color = ~cohort,
      colors = CVM_COHORT_COLORS, type = "scatter", mode = "lines+markers"
    ) |>
      plotly::layout(
        xaxis = list(title = "Period order within cohort"),
        yaxis = list(title = "Median grade (%)", range = c(0, 100)),
        paper_bgcolor = "white", plot_bgcolor = "white",
        font = list(color = "#1A1A2E")
      )
  }
)
```

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Summary by cohort"
tbl <- build_summary_table(all_data)
tagList(
  ui_section_header("Cohort-by-cohort numbers"),
  DT::datatable(
    tbl, rownames = FALSE,
    options = list(dom = "Bfrtip", buttons = c("csv"),
                   pageLength = 10, scrollX = TRUE),
    extensions = "Buttons"
  )
)
```

## Row

```{r}
#| title: "Change narrative"
td <- build_tag_delta(all_data)
cn <- change_narrative(td)

card <- function(title, items, klass) {
  tags$div(class = paste("cvm-flag", klass), style = "margin-bottom:12px;",
    tags$div(
      tags$div(class = "label", htmlEscape(title)),
      if (length(items) > 0) {
        tags$ul(lapply(items, function(x) tags$li(htmlEscape(x))))
      } else {
        tags$p(style = "color:#5A6472;", "No changes to report.")
      }
    )
  )
}

tagList(
  ui_section_header("What shifted between the earliest and latest cohort"),
  tags$div(class = "cvm-change-cards",
    card("Most improved tags",  cn$improved,   "green"),
    card("Tags at risk",         cn$at_risk,    "red"),
    card("Consistent strengths", cn$consistent, "amber")
  )
)
```

# Curriculum Tag Coverage

```{r}
#| output: asis
# See course_year_dashboard.qmd for why bare htmltools tags need the
# `{=html}` raw-HTML fence. TL;DR: without it, pandoc re-parses <span> tags
# as markdown inline text and they render as literal source.
emit_html <- function(tag) {
  if (is.null(tag)) return(invisible())
  cat("\n```{=html}\n", as.character(tag), "\n```\n\n", sep = "")
}

cat("## {.tabset}\n\n")
for (r in all_data) {
  cat(sprintf("### %s\n\n", r$cohort_label))

  # Wrap the whole per-cohort block in a .cvm-filterable div, same pattern
  # as the Assessment Performance chunk.
  keys <- cohort_period_keys(r)
  emit_html(HTML(sprintf(
    '<div class="cvm-filterable" data-period-keys="%s">',
    htmlEscape(keys)
  )))

  tc <- r$tag_coverage
  first <- if (!is.null(tc) && length(tc) > 0) tc[[1]] else NULL
  if (is.null(first) || length(first) == 0) {
    emit_html(ui_banner(
      sprintf("No tag coverage data for %s.", htmlEscape(r$cohort_label)),
      variant = "info"
    ))
    emit_html(HTML("</div>"))
    next
  }
  cat("#### {.tabset}\n\n")
  for (ts in names(first)) {
    pretty <- pretty_tagset_name(ts)
    cat(sprintf("##### %s\n\n", pretty))
    dfs    <- lapply(tc, function(x) x[[ts]])
    merged <- dplyr::bind_rows(Filter(Negate(is.null), dfs))
    s      <- tag_coverage_summary(merged)
    bar    <- tag_bar_plotly(merged, title = pretty)

    emit_html(ui_coverage_strip(
      s$covered, s$total, s$pct,
      label = sprintf("%s covered", tolower(pretty))
    ))

    if (!is.null(r$tagset_plots[[ts]])) {
      emit_html(ui_section_header("Coverage chart"))
      print(plotly::ggplotly(r$tagset_plots[[ts]]) |>
              plotly::config(displaylogo = FALSE))
    }

    emit_html(ui_section_header("Tag-by-tag counts"))
    print(bar$plot)

    emit_html(ui_deprecated_note(bar$deprecated_count))

    emit_html(ui_section_header(sprintf("Uncovered tags (%d)", length(s$uncovered))))
    emit_html(ui_tag_chips(s$uncovered))
  }
  # Close the .cvm-filterable wrapper for this cohort.
  emit_html(HTML("</div>"))
}
```

# Combined Narrative

```{r}
narr_path <- sub("\\.rds$", "_narrative.rds", params$all_data_path)
narrative <- if (file.exists(narr_path)) readRDS(narr_path) else NULL

if (is.null(narrative)) {
  HTML('<div class="cvm-banner info">Narrative not generated for this render.</div>')
} else if (isFALSE(narrative$available %||% TRUE)) {
  HTML(paste0(
    '<div class="cvm-narrative"><p>',
    htmlEscape(narrative$narrative), '</p></div>'
  ))
} else {
  flags_ui <- if (!is.null(narrative$flags) && nrow(narrative$flags) > 0) {
    tagList(lapply(seq_len(nrow(narrative$flags)), function(i) {
      f <- narrative$flags[i, ]
      klass <- switch(f$severity,
                      high   = "cvm-flag red",
                      medium = "cvm-flag amber",
                      low    = "cvm-flag green",
                      "cvm-flag amber")
      tags$div(class = klass,
        tags$div(
          tags$div(class = "label", toupper(f$severity)),
          tags$div(class = "issue", f$issue),
          tags$div(class = "rec",   f$recommendation)
        )
      )
    }))
  } else NULL

  questions_ui <- if (length(narrative$questions) > 0) {
    tags$ol(class = "cvm-questions", lapply(narrative$questions, tags$li))
  } else NULL

  tagList(
    tags$div(class = "cvm-narrative", HTML(markdown_to_html(narrative$narrative))),
    if (!is.null(flags_ui))     tagList(ui_section_header("Areas of concern"),     flags_ui),
    if (!is.null(questions_ui)) tagList(ui_section_header("Questions to explore"), questions_ui),
    tags$p(
      style = "color:#5A6472; font-size:12px; margin-top:24px;",
      sprintf("Generated %s using %s.",
              fmt_ts(narrative$generated_at),
              narrative$model %||% "—")
    )
  )
}
```

```{r}
#| include: false
#| output: false
# Serialize the filter-bar data and the bootstrap JS. This chunk intentionally
# produces no visible output — the raw {=html} block below is what lands on
# the page.
```

```{=html}
<script id="cvm-dashboard-data" type="application/json">
`r period_index_json`
</script>
<style>
  /* Filter bar + state styling. Intentionally scoped via .cvm-* classes so it
     doesn't collide with Quarto's Bootstrap layout. */
  .cvm-filter-bar {
    position: sticky;
    top: 0;
    z-index: 900;
    background: #FFFFFF;
    box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.08);
    padding: 12px 24px;
    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 16px; flex-wrap: wrap;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #E0E4EC;
    font-family: Inter, "Source Sans Pro", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
  }
  .cvm-filter-bar .label {
    font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600;
    text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em;
    color: #5A6472;
  }
  .cvm-filter-bar .chips { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; }
  .cvm-chip {
    display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px;
    padding: 6px 14px;
    border: 1px solid #003F6B;
    border-radius: 20px;
    background: #FFFFFF;
    color: #003F6B;
    font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500;
    cursor: pointer; user-select: none;
    transition: background 120ms, color 120ms;
  }
  .cvm-chip:hover { background: #EEF4FB; }
  .cvm-chip.active { background: #003F6B; color: #FFFFFF; }
  .cvm-chip:focus-visible {
    outline: 2px solid #AB8B00; outline-offset: 2px;
  }
  .cvm-filter-actions { margin-left: auto; display: flex; gap: 14px; }
  .cvm-filter-actions a {
    color: #AB8B00; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500;
    cursor: pointer; text-decoration: none;
    border-bottom: 1px dashed rgba(171,139,0,0.4);
  }
  .cvm-filter-actions a:hover { color: #003F6B; border-color: #003F6B; }
  .cvm-filterable { transition: opacity 150ms; }
</style>
<script>
(function() {
  // ---- data -------------------------------------------------------------
  const dataEl = document.getElementById('cvm-dashboard-data');
  if (!dataEl) return;
  let PERIODS;
  try { PERIODS = JSON.parse(dataEl.textContent); }
  catch (e) { console.error('CVM filter: could not parse data', e); return; }
  if (!Array.isArray(PERIODS) || PERIODS.length === 0) return;

  const STORAGE_KEY = 'cvm-dashboard-filter';
  const ALL_IDS = PERIODS.map(p => p.id);

  // ---- state ------------------------------------------------------------
  let active;
  try {
    const saved = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY) || 'null');
    active = Array.isArray(saved)
      ? new Set(saved.filter(id => ALL_IDS.includes(id)))
      : new Set(ALL_IDS);
    if (active.size === 0) active = new Set(ALL_IDS);
  } catch (e) { active = new Set(ALL_IDS); }

  function persist() {
    try { localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify([...active])); }
    catch (e) { /* private-mode etc — ignore */ }
  }

  // ---- helpers ----------------------------------------------------------
  function median(xs) {
    if (xs.length === 0) return null;
    const s = [...xs].sort((a,b) => a - b);
    const m = Math.floor(s.length / 2);
    return s.length % 2 ? s[m] : (s[m-1] + s[m]) / 2;
  }
  function selectedPeriods() {
    return PERIODS.filter(p => active.has(p.id));
  }
  function recomputeKpis() {
    const sel = selectedPeriods();
    const medians = sel.map(p => p.median).filter(v => typeof v === 'number');
    const students = sel.reduce((s, p) => s + (p.students || 0), 0);
    const periods  = sel.length;
    const tagSets  = sel.reduce((m, p) => Math.max(m, p.n_tag_sets || 0), 0);

    setKpi('median',   medians.length ? median(medians).toFixed(1) : '—');
    setKpi('students', students);
    setKpi('periods',  periods);
    setKpi('tag_sets', tagSets);
  }
  function setKpi(key, val) {
    document.querySelectorAll('[data-kpi="' + key + '"] .cvm-kpi-value').forEach(el => {
      el.textContent = String(val);
    });
  }
  function apply() {
    // Chip visual state
    document.querySelectorAll('.cvm-chip').forEach(chip => {
      chip.classList.toggle('active', active.has(chip.dataset.periodId));
      chip.setAttribute('aria-pressed', active.has(chip.dataset.periodId) ? 'true' : 'false');
    });
    // Filterable content blocks
    document.querySelectorAll('.cvm-filterable').forEach(el => {
      const keys = (el.dataset.periodKeys || '').split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean);
      const match = keys.length === 0 ? true : keys.some(k => active.has(k));
      el.style.display = match ? '' : 'none';
    });
    recomputeKpis();
  }

  // ---- build the bar ----------------------------------------------------
  function build() {
    const nav = document.querySelector('.quarto-dashboard .navbar')
             || document.querySelector('header.navbar')
             || document.querySelector('nav');
    if (!nav) { console.warn('CVM filter: no navbar anchor found'); return; }

    const bar = document.createElement('div');
    bar.className = 'cvm-filter-bar';
    bar.setAttribute('role', 'toolbar');
    bar.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Filter by graduating class and period');

    const label = document.createElement('div');
    label.className = 'label';
    label.textContent = 'Periods';
    bar.appendChild(label);

    const chipsWrap = document.createElement('div');
    chipsWrap.className = 'chips';
    PERIODS.forEach(p => {
      const chip = document.createElement('button');
      chip.type = 'button';
      chip.className = 'cvm-chip';
      chip.dataset.periodId = p.id;
      chip.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');
      chip.textContent = p.label;
      chip.addEventListener('click', () => {
        if (active.has(p.id)) active.delete(p.id);
        else active.add(p.id);
        if (active.size === 0) active = new Set(ALL_IDS);
        persist(); apply();
      });
      chipsWrap.appendChild(chip);
    });
    bar.appendChild(chipsWrap);

    const actions = document.createElement('div');
    actions.className = 'cvm-filter-actions';
    const selAll = document.createElement('a');
    selAll.textContent = 'Select all';
    selAll.setAttribute('role', 'button'); selAll.setAttribute('tabindex', '0');
    selAll.addEventListener('click', () => { active = new Set(ALL_IDS); persist(); apply(); });
    const clear = document.createElement('a');
    clear.textContent = 'Clear';
    clear.setAttribute('role', 'button'); clear.setAttribute('tabindex', '0');
    clear.addEventListener('click', () => {
      // "Clear" is really "single-period view"-lite: empty => reset to all.
      active = new Set(); persist(); apply();
      setTimeout(() => {
        if (active.size === 0) { active = new Set(ALL_IDS); persist(); apply(); }
      }, 3000);
    });
    actions.appendChild(selAll); actions.appendChild(clear);
    bar.appendChild(actions);

    nav.parentNode.insertBefore(bar, nav.nextSibling);
  }

  // ---- init ------------------------------------------------------------
  // Multiple triggers because Quarto's dashboard layout JS can reorder DOM
  // after DOMContentLoaded — apply() needs to run AFTER the final DOM state.
  // Each trigger wrapped in try/catch so one failure doesn't kill the others.
  let hasInit = false;
  function runOnce() {
    if (hasInit) { try { apply(); } catch (e) { console.error('CVM filter: apply() failed', e); } return; }
    hasInit = true;
    try { build(); } catch (e) { console.error('CVM filter: build() failed', e); }
    try {
      apply();
      console.log('CVM filter: initial apply OK, ' + PERIODS.length + ' periods, active=' + active.size);
      // Verify KPI elements exist — log a warning if not (usually means DOM
      // wasn't ready or selectors don't match Quarto's actual output).
      ['median','students','periods','tag_sets'].forEach(k => {
        const n = document.querySelectorAll('[data-kpi="' + k + '"] .cvm-kpi-value').length;
        if (n === 0) console.warn('CVM filter: no element for data-kpi="' + k + '"');
      });
    } catch (e) { console.error('CVM filter: apply() failed', e); }
  }

  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', runOnce);
  } else {
    runOnce();
  }
  // Second call on window.load — catches DOM changes made after
  // DOMContentLoaded by Quarto's dashboard JS or htmlwidgets.
  window.addEventListener('load', () => { try { apply(); } catch (e) { console.error('CVM filter: window.load apply() failed', e); } });
  // Third call on a 300ms timer — belt and suspenders for late dom mutations.
  setTimeout(() => { try { apply(); } catch (e) { /* swallow */ } }, 300);
})();
</script>
```
